#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

### Post by RomeReactor on 2008-02-16
**doctorbighands**, if **SKIP_CHECKS=yes compiz** didn't work, and you still have Ubuntu-Desktop installed, try logging out and back in to it, and see if desktop effects can now be enabled.

**kaustubh.cool**, do you have the ATI drivers installed? if not, or you're unsure, go to 'System->Administration->Restricted Drivers Manager' and check the box for your card. Also, try installing installing **xserver-xgl**; look for it in Synaptic (System->Administration->Synaptic) or from the terminal (Applications->Accessories->Terminal):
```
sudo aptitude install xserver-xgl
```
then reboot and see if yo can enable the effects.

---

### Post by doctorbighands on 2008-02-16
This IS a problem with Ubuntu Studio!! I can't believe it!

I entered "sudo apt-get install ubuntu-desktop" into a terminal, and it told me it couldn't download that because it depends on "ubuntu-sounds." In order to install the Ubuntu Studio theme package, I had to REMOVE ubuntu-sounds, which means ubuntu-desktop went with it!

NOW what do I do? I really want to keep the Studio theme...does all of this mean I won't be able to?

---

### Post by RomeReactor on 2008-02-16
You don't need the full ubuntustudio-desktop to get the theme; you can reinstall ubuntu-desktop, along with **ubuntustudio-theme**:
```
sudo aptitude install ubuntu-desktop ubuntustudio-theme ubuntustudio-icon-theme
```

---

It seems I can't have it both ways. I wish I could...

### Post by doctorbighands on 2008-02-16

### Post by RomeReactor on 2008-02-16
This isn't an issue with the theme, but rather with the session you're running; ubuntu-desktop is a metapackage that points the installer to the necessary packages for the complete desktop, and in itself isn't really necessary (though it *is* recommended you keep it installed for smooth updates). In any case, even if the ubuntu-desktop package is not installed, the actual ubunt dekstop **is**. So try logging out and then back in, but in the log in screen go to 'Options' (or 'Sessions') and select the Ubuntu session, not Ubuntu Studio.

What error messages do you get when you run compiz from the terminal?

---

Checking for texture_from_pixmap: not present.

### Post by RomeReactor on 2008-02-16
Try switching to the i810 driver; open a terminal and run:
```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup
```
to make a backup off your xorg.conf, just as a precaution; then go to 'System->Administration->Screens and Graphics' and on the second tab (Graphics Card) select the **i810** driver. Afterwards, press CTRL+ALT+BACKSPACE to restart the display, and see if you can enable the effects.

NOTE: After changing the driver and restarting your display, post the contents of your xorg.conf:
```
cat /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

If for any reason you want to revert to your previous xorg configuration, run:
```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```
and again press CTRL+ALT+BACKSPACE to restart X.

### Post by Kivech on 2008-02-16
Ok, you are not the only one having problems activating the special effects of compiz.

I managed to get mine going with these 'simple' instructions. They for sure are not going to hurt anything, so you might as well give it a try.

Follow the instructions of cifrancgx about halfway this page [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=436110&page=2](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=436110&page=2) 

Really, in my case I had tried it all, but this fixed it just like that. And if it's all the same, I did a clean reinstall after I found the method that worked, since I tried so many and couldn't for the life of me trace back what changes I made where. So if that's not too much hassle for you, I certainly recommend that. Just to prevent weird things showing up in the future. Being a complete newbie to this, I just want to make sure I don't get any nasty surprizes in the future.

In case it doesn't work, have a look at the Desktop Effects & Customization section of the Ubuntu forums. There's litterally dozens of threads like these in there with all varying problems and solutions.

Good luck!

Kivech

### Post by doctorbighands on 2008-02-16
I just re-installed Hardy, and now everything is working as it should.

I don't know what the deal is with Gutsy on HP laptops, but it's just buggy as hell. I've tried Edgy, Feisty, and now Hardy all on this same laptop, and I haven't run into half the problems I ever did with Gutsy (and, lest we forget, Hardy is still alpha!). My wife's Dell machine runs Gutsy like a dream - she loves it! I guess I'm just very thankful for version 8. :)

Thank you all for your help on this.
